scope.summary: Cluster H closed on the coverage-that-can-fail lens rather than a test count: all 14 children are closed, and a sweep for tests that would pass with their subject broken found 17 CI/e2e gates that silently pass when their name filter matches zero tests. All 17 now fail closed.
out_of_scope: Plan §30.4 items 2 (DI1 held-execute) and 3 (MET cardinality). Those regressions are owned by Cluster F beads, not Cluster H; oraclemcp-f-server-met-bounded-tool-labels-na6y is still open. Cluster H's own 14 children are all closed.
The `--test <binary>` and `--lib` selectors in the conformance matrix: cargo errors when a target is missing, so they cannot silently match zero and were deliberately left alone.

Vacuous-coverage classes other than the zero-match filter (for example assertion-free tests, or goldens that regenerate themselves). §30.5's self-fulfilling-fixture rule and scripts/oraclemcp_fixture_lint.sh cover part of that ground; this close does not claim to have swept it.

entry_points.name: the finding: `cargo test <filter>` exits 0 on zero matches
entry_points.trace: Demonstrated, not assumed: `cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit --lib a_name_that_matches_no_test_whatsoever` prints 'test result: ok. 0 passed; 0 failed; 244 filtered out' and exits 0. 17 invocations across 4 scripts trusted that exit status with a hard-coded filter and none checked that a test ran, so each would keep passing while asserting nothing after a rename. The CI-wired one (oraclemcp_ergonomics_lint.sh) gates the agent-facing CLI contract.

entry_points.name: e2e_cargo_test_filter (scripts/e2e/lib.sh)
entry_points.trace: Sums 'N passed' across every binary an invocation ran and fails below a caller-declared minimum. Proven in both directions by a standalone probe: a zero-match filter is refused with 'filter matched 0 test(s), expected at least 1', and a real filter (no_variant_name_prefixes_another) exits 0. Wired into 16 gates; the 17th (the ergonomics lint) carries the same check inlined because it has its own documented exit codes and no e2e-harness dependency, and that inlined logic was exercised through an identical code path in both directions.

limitations: The e2e scripts could not be run to green in this working tree: oraclemcp-db does not compile because another pane's in-flight logoff work references EXPLICIT_LOGOFF_CLOSED_REASON at connection.rs:5486 with no definition in scope. That is uncommitted work, not HEAD. The guard's behaviour is therefore evidenced by the standalone both-directions probe plus a dry run in which it fires with its own message, not by a full green e2e pass.
The ergonomics lint's real invocation could not be executed either: -p oraclemcp does not build while another pane holds ~1081 uncommitted lines in dispatch/, main.rs and registry.rs.
A fixed minimum of 1 (2 for the ergonomics drift guards) is a floor, not an exact count: it catches deletion and rename, not a test that was silently narrowed from many cases to one.
Cluster H is closed on its own 14 children plus this sweep. It does not assert anything about the Cluster F regressions listed in out_of_scope.
